# Implementation Plan: Wire Adapters to AsyncReflectiveMutationProposer

**Branch**: `016-wire-adapters-proposer` | **Date**: 2026-01-12 | **Spec**: [spec.md](./spec.md)
**Input**: Feature specification from `/specs/016-wire-adapters-proposer/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan was generated by the `/speckit.plan` command.

## Summary

Enable ADKAdapter and MultiAgentAdapter to generate actual instruction mutations via LLM by wiring them to `AsyncReflectiveMutationProposer`. Both adapters will accept an optional `proposer` parameter with automatic default instantiation, replacing the current stub (ADKAdapter) and heuristic-based (MultiAgentAdapter) implementations with proper delegation to the proposer's `propose()` method.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: Python 3.12  
**Primary Dependencies**: google-adk>=1.22.0, litellm>=1.80.13, structlog>=25.5.0  
**Storage**: N/A (in-memory state only)  
**Testing**: pytest with three-layer strategy (contract/unit/integration)  
**Target Platform**: Linux server, cross-platform CLI  
**Project Type**: Single Python package with hexagonal architecture  
**Performance Goals**: Async-first for concurrent evaluation (3-5x performance)  
**Constraints**: Backward compatible (existing tests must pass)  
**Scale/Scope**: Small feature (~100 LOC changes across 2 adapter files + tests)

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

| Principle | Status | Notes |
|-----------|--------|-------|
| **I. Hexagonal Architecture** | ✅ PASS | Proposer is in `engine/`, adapters in `adapters/`. Engine receives proposer via injection (FR-001, FR-002). No new layer violations. |
| **II. Async-First Design** | ✅ PASS | `propose_new_texts()` already async; `proposer.propose()` is async. No sync/async bridging needed. |
| **III. Protocol-Based Interfaces** | ✅ PASS | `AsyncReflectiveMutationProposer` is a concrete class (not protocol), but injection follows dependency inversion. No protocol changes needed. |
| **IV. Three-Layer Testing** | ✅ PASS | Tests required: unit tests for delegation logic, contract tests for adapter protocol compliance (already exist). |
| **V. Observability & Documentation** | ✅ PASS | FR-008 requires logging; FR-009 requires docstring updates. Existing structlog patterns apply. |

**Gate Result**: ✅ All principles satisfied. Proceed to Phase 0.

### Source Code (repository root)

```text
src/gepa_adk/
├── adapters/
│   ├── adk_adapter.py      # MODIFY: Add proposer parameter, wire propose_new_texts
│   └── multi_agent.py      # MODIFY: Add proposer parameter, wire propose_new_texts
├── engine/
│   └── proposer.py         # EXISTING: AsyncReflectiveMutationProposer (no changes)
├── domain/                 # No changes
├── ports/                  # No changes
└── utils/                  # No changes

tests/
├── contracts/              # Existing adapter protocol tests (verify still pass)
├── integration/            # No changes (LLM integration tests exist)
└── unit/
    └── adapters/
        ├── test_adk_adapter.py           # MODIFY: Add proposer delegation tests
        └── test_multi_agent_adapter.py   # MODIFY: Add proposer delegation tests
```

**Structure Decision**: Single project structure, modifying existing adapter files. No new directories needed. Follows established hexagonal architecture with adapters depending on engine via constructor injection.

## Complexity Tracking

> No constitution violations. No complexity tracking needed.

## Post-Design Constitution Check

*Re-evaluation after Phase 1 design artifacts created.*

| Principle | Status | Post-Design Notes |
|-----------|--------|-------------------|
| **I. Hexagonal Architecture** | ✅ PASS | Import `AsyncReflectiveMutationProposer` from engine→adapters is valid per layer rules. No protocol extraction needed (YAGNI). |
| **II. Async-First Design** | ✅ PASS | Delegation uses `await self._proposer.propose()`. No blocking calls introduced. |
| **III. Protocol-Based Interfaces** | ✅ PASS | Using concrete class injection is acceptable for internal dependencies. Protocol can be extracted later if needed. |
| **IV. Three-Layer Testing** | ✅ PASS | Unit tests use `AsyncMock` for proposer. No integration test changes needed (proposer already tested). |
| **V. Observability & Documentation** | ✅ PASS | Logging events defined in contracts. Docstrings documented in quickstart examples. |

**Post-Design Gate Result**: ✅ All principles satisfied. Ready for Phase 2 task generation.
